basics.description: **Note:** Please refer to the following before installing the solution: • Review the solution [Release Notes](https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel/tree/master/Solutions/AbnormalSecurity/ReleaseNotes.md) • There may be [known issues](https://aka.ms/sentinelsolutionsknownissues) pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing. The Abnormal Security solution provides real-time security event ingestion from Abnormal's cloud email security platform into Microsoft Sentinel. Supports both push-based (CCF Push) and pull-based (Azure Functions) connectors. The push connector routes events to per-event-type tables (threats, cases, audit logs, abuse mailbox, posture changes, ATO cases, remediations, vendor cases) using the Codeless Connector Framework. **Data Connectors:** 2, **Parsers:** 4, **Workbooks:** 1, **Analytic Rules:** 4, **Hunting Queries:** 4, **Playbooks:** 1 [Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel](https://aka.ms/azuresentinel) | [Learn more about Solutions](https://aka.ms/azuresentinelsolutionsdoc)

metadata.hidden: Hiding location, we get it from the log analytics workspace

basics.toolTip: This filters by workspaces that exist in the Resource Group selected

basics.name: workspace
basics.label: Workspace
basics.toolTip: This dropdown will list only workspace that exists in the Resource Group selected

options.text: This Solution installs the data connector for Abnormal Security (Push). You can get Abnormal Security (Push) data in your Microsoft Sentinel workspace. After installing the solution, configure and enable this data connector by following guidance in Manage solution view.

link.label: Learn more about connecting data sources

options.text: This Solution installs the data connector for AbnormalSecurity. You can get AbnormalSecurity custom log data in your Microsoft Sentinel workspace. After installing the solution, configure and enable this data connector by following guidance in Manage solution view.

The Solution installs a parser that transforms the ingested data into Microsoft Sentinel normalized format. The normalized format enables better correlation of different types of data from different data sources to drive end-to-end outcomes seamlessly in security monitoring, hunting, incident investigation and response scenarios in Microsoft Sentinel.

link.label: Learn more about connecting data sources

options.text: Table naming: by default the Push connector creates its tables with a "_V2" suffix (e.g. ABNORMAL_SECURITY_THREAT_LOG_V2_CL). Keep this ON so the connector creates its own tables and will not conflict with tables from a previous Abnormal Security connector on this workspace — a conflict otherwise makes the Deploy step fail with 'Invalid output table schema'. Turn it OFF only if you are updating an existing installation that already uses the non-suffixed table names.

elements.name: useV2Tables
elements.label: Append a "_V2" suffix to the Abnormal Security table names
elements.toolTip: Creates tables as ABNORMAL_SECURITY_..._V2_CL to avoid conflicts with an existing Abnormal connector on this workspace.

options.text: This Microsoft Sentinel Solution installs workbooks. Workbooks provide a flexible canvas for data monitoring, analysis, and the creation of rich visual reports within the Azure portal. They allow you to tap into one or many data sources from Microsoft Sentinel and combine them into unified interactive experiences.
Summarizes email threats, account takeover cases, user-reported messages, and vendor cases ingested from the Abnormal Security CCF Push connector.
This solution installs the following analytic rule templates. After installing the solution, create and enable analytic rules in Manage solution view.

elements.name: analytic1
elements.label: Abnormal Security - High-risk email attack detected

options.text:
Identifies email attacks detected by Abnormal Security whose attack type maps to a
high-risk category (credential phishing, Business Email Compromise, invoice/payment
fraud, malware, extortion, sensitive-data phishing, internal account-takeover attacks,
or scams). Lower-risk categories such as Spam, Graymail, and Reconnaissance are
intentionally excluded. Use this to triage targeted email threats that reached a mailbox.

elements.name: analytic2
elements.label: Abnormal Security - Account Takeover case opened

options.text:
Creates an incident when Abnormal Security opens an Account Takeover (ATO) case,
indicating a mailbox or user account is suspected to be compromised. The ATO case
severity, status, and observed indicators are surfaced for triage.

elements.name: analytic3
elements.label: Abnormal Security - User-reported email judged malicious

options.text:
Creates an incident when an email a user reported to the Abnormal Security Abuse
Mailbox is judged malicious. Surfaces the reporter, the original recipient, and the
campaign so analysts can scope and remediate look-alike messages. Spam- and safe-judged
reports are intentionally excluded.

elements.name: analytic4
elements.label: Abnormal Security - Vendor compromise case detected

options.text:
Creates an incident when Abnormal Security opens a vendor case indicating a
compromised or impersonated vendor domain (vendor email compromise). Surfaces the
vendor domain and Abnormal insights so analysts can review related correspondence.
This solution installs the following hunting queries. After installing the solution, run these hunting queries to hunt for threats in Manage solution view.

elements.name: huntingquery1
elements.label: Abnormal Security - Threats still in the mailbox

options.text:
Surfaces Abnormal Security threats whose remediation status indicates the message
has not been removed from the mailbox (not auto-remediated and not post-remediated).
Hunt for attacks that may still be reachable by recipients. This hunting query depends on AbnormalSecurityPush data connector (ABNORMAL_SECURITY_THREAT_LOG_CL Parser or Table)

Aggregates Abnormal Security threats by recipient to reveal the most-targeted
users over the hunting window. A spike for a single recipient can indicate a
focused campaign against a high-value target. This hunting query depends on AbnormalSecurityPush data connector (ABNORMAL_SECURITY_THREAT_LOG_CL Parser or Table)

Groups user-reported Abuse Mailbox submissions by campaign to show which reported
campaigns affected the most recipients. Useful to gauge the spread of a phishing
campaign that employees flagged. This hunting query depends on AbnormalSecurityPush data connector (ABNORMAL_SECURITY_ABUSE_MAILBOX_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery4
elements.label: Abnormal Security - Newly observed vendor domains

options.text:
Lists vendor domains that first appear in Abnormal Security vendor cases within the
hunting window. A newly observed vendor domain tied to a case can be an early signal
of vendor email compromise or impersonation. This hunting query depends on AbnormalSecurityPush data connector (ABNORMAL_SECURITY_VENDOR_CASE_CL Parser or Table)
This solution installs the following Playbook templates. After installing the solution, playbooks can be managed in the Manage solution view.
